title: Joint Compute and Layout Optimization via Hierarchical E-Graphs
description: We propose MemSat, a compiler optimization framework that combines hierarchical equality saturation with joint compute and data layout optimization. By representing both computation and layout transformations in a multi-level e-graph (loop nest → function → module) and leveraging treewidth-aware extraction algorithms, MemSat enables joint optimization of compute and memory bandwidth with near-optimal solution quality and practical compile times.
motivation: Modern compilers face the phase-ordering problem where optimization pass sequences dramatically affect output quality. Equality saturation eliminates this by representing all equivalent program variants in an e-graph. However, existing approaches (1) use slow ILP or suboptimal greedy extraction, and (2) ignore data layout optimization despite memory bandwidth being the dominant performance bottleneck. Recent treewidth-aware extraction algorithms (Goharshady et al. 2024; Sun et al. 2024) make optimal extraction practical, but have only been applied to extraction in isolation, not to joint compute+layout optimization.
proposed_approach: MemSat introduces three key innovations: (1) Hierarchical e-graphs that enable compositional equality saturation at loop nest, function, and module levels, keeping treewidth bounded; (2) Layout-aware rewrite rules that embed data layout transformations (AoS/SoA, tiling, padding) directly into the e-graph with memory cost models, verified correct via structural equivalence proofs; (3) Application of existing treewidth-aware extraction algorithms to this hierarchical representation for near-optimal solutions. Our contribution is the system architecture enabling joint optimization, not the extraction algorithms themselves.

related_work.title: egg: Fast and Extensible Equality Saturation
related_work.authors: Max Willsey, Chandrakana Nandi, Yisu Remy Wang, Oliver Flatt, Zachary Tatlock, Pavel Panchekha
related_work.contribution: Introduced the egg library that made equality saturation accessible
related_work.difference: We focus on hierarchical representation and joint compute+layout optimization, not the core e-graph infrastructure

related_work.title: Fast and Optimal Extraction for Sparse Equality Graphs
related_work.authors: Amir Kafshdar Goharshady, Chun Kit Lam, Lionel Parreaux
related_work.contribution: Showed real-world e-graphs have low treewidth; presented parameterized extraction algorithm
related_work.difference: They apply extraction to Cranelift benchmarks in isolation; we use hierarchical e-graphs for joint compute+layout optimization

related_work.title: E-Graphs as Circuits, and Optimal Extraction via Treewidth
related_work.authors: Glenn Sun, Yihong Zhang, Haobin Ni
related_work.contribution: Established e-graph to Boolean circuit connection; derived treewidth-parameterized extraction with simplification techniques
related_work.difference: They focus on circuit view and simplification; we focus on hierarchical decomposition and layout optimization

related_work.title: Equality Saturation for Tensor Graph Superoptimization (TenSat)
related_work.authors: Yichen Yang, Phitchaya Mangpo Phothilimthana, Yisu Remy Wang, Max Willsey, Sudip Roy, Jacques Pienaar
related_work.contribution: Applied equality saturation to tensor computation graphs with ILP extraction
related_work.difference: TenSat uses ILP extraction and doesn't consider data layout transformations; we use treewidth-aware extraction and joint layout optimization

related_work.title: AutoPhase: Juggling HLS Phase Orderings in Random Forests with Deep Reinforcement Learning
related_work.authors: Ameer Haj-Ali, Qijing Huang, William Moses, John Xiang, Krste Asanovic, John Wawrzynek, Ion Stoica
related_work.contribution: Uses RL to find compiler pass orderings for HLS
related_work.difference: ML-guided phase ordering requires training and doesn't eliminate phase ordering; we use equality saturation which eliminates it by construction

related_work.title: Solving the Phase Ordering Problem ≠ Generating the Globally Optimal Code
related_work.authors: Yu Wang, Hongyu Chen, Ke Wang
related_work.contribution: Shows that solving phase ordering doesn't guarantee globally optimal code; proposes bi-directional optimization
related_work.difference: They explore less efficient intermediate programs via reverse optimizations; we use equality saturation to explore all equivalent programs simultaneously

related_work.title: Mind the Abstraction Gap: Bringing Equality Saturation to Real-World ML Compilers
related_work.authors: Arya Vohra, Leo Seojun Lee, Jakub Bachurski, Oleksandr Zinenko, Phitchaya Mangpo Phothilimthana, Albert Cohen, William S. Moses
related_work.contribution: Bridges gap between e-graphs and production MLIR-based compilers
related_work.difference: They focus on compiler integration; we focus on joint compute+layout optimization via hierarchical decomposition

related_work.title: A Practical Automatic Polyhedral Parallelizer and Locality Optimizer
related_work.authors: Uday Bondhugula, Albert Hartono, J. Ramanujam, P. Sadayappan
related_work.contribution: Pluto: polyhedral loop transformations for parallelism and locality
related_work.difference: Polyhedral compilation focuses on loop transformations; we integrate layout optimization into equality saturation

hypothesis: H1: Real-world program e-graphs exhibit low treewidth (≤10) when rewrite rules are applied hierarchically, making treewidth-aware extraction practical.
H2: Joint optimization of computation and data layout via unified hierarchical e-graph representation achieves better memory bandwidth utilization than sequential optimization.
H3: The combination of hierarchical saturation and treewidth-aware extraction provides compile times competitive with greedy extraction while achieving solution quality within 10% of optimal ILP.

confirmed_if: At least 70% of benchmark e-graphs have treewidth ≤ 10 under hierarchical decomposition
MemSat achieves ≥10% geomean speedup over LLVM -O3 on memory-bound workloads
Extraction is ≤5× slower than greedy while achieving ≥90% of ILP solution quality
refuted_if: Real-world e-graphs have treewidth > 15 under hierarchical decomposition
Joint optimization shows no improvement over sequential (computation then layout)
Hierarchical decomposition loses optimization opportunities vs. flat e-graphs

novelty_claims: First framework combining hierarchical equality saturation with joint compute and data layout optimization
Hierarchical e-graph representation with bounded treewidth for compositional optimization
Verified layout transformation rules with formal soundness guarantees
limitations: 8-hour time limit restricts implementation scope—may need beam search fallback instead of full treewidth-aware extraction
Initial prototype focuses on Polybench kernels, not full applications
